title: Connections, Channel Ownership, Heartbeats, and Recovery · Execution Trace: Channel Ownership
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must assign one recovery owner, reject stale generations, drain notifications, and test amqp091-go 1.12 recovery limits; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the channel ownership increment in a cancellable connection and channel recovery state machine. Learners p…
objectives: Assign channel ownership by publisher or consumer lifecycle and prevent concurrent confirm, acknowledgement, and topology operations from corrupting assumptions; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use bounded long-lived AMQP connections and explain multiplexed channels, negotiated limits, names, TLS, and lifecycle cost; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Channel Ownership · Theory Channel Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 5444 during quorum failover exercise during a rolling broker restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound heartbeat and recovery delay while rejecting stale channel-generation notifications; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
